task_completion.description: Open the patient record for John Zenon
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GNU Health interface should display the patient record or dashboard specifically for 'John Zenon'.

task_completion.description: Document a clinical evaluation with a low-grade fever
task_completion.visual_evidence: An evaluation, clinical encounter, or vital signs form showing the patient's temperature recorded as 37.5 C or higher.
A condition, diagnosis, or pathology entry in the patient's record showing 'Lyme disease' and/or the ICD-10 code 'A69.2'.
A medication prescription or treatment order for 'Doxycycline', 'Amoxicillin', or 'Cefuroxime' associated with the patient.

task_completion.description: Order at least one relevant laboratory test
task_completion.visual_evidence: A laboratory test request (e.g., Lyme Serology, CBC, or similar blood test) created for the patient.

task_completion.description: Schedule a follow-up appointment 14 to 28 days from today
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment scheduled in the GNU Health calendar or patient appointments list, with a date that is 14 to 28 days after the current system date.

integrity.description: Agent used the GNU Health graphical user interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the GNU Health desktop client windows, menus, and forms, rather than terminal commands or database prompts.

integrity.description: Agent entered data into the actual application fields
integrity.visual_evidence: Text and selections should be visible inside the proper GNU Health input fields, dropdowns, and dialog boxes, not in a separate text editor.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database queries to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent opening a terminal and using 'psql' or python scripts to insert records directly into the GNU Health database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient Name: John Zenon. Diagnosis: Lyme disease (ICD-10: A69.2). Minimum Temperature: 37.5 C. Acceptable Antibiotics: Doxycycline, Amoxicillin, or Cefuroxime. Minimum Lab Orders: 1. Follow-up Appointment: 14 to 28 days from the current date.
